snippet: The Designated Bilingual Program (DBP) provides annual funding to support the delivery of bilingual education across a select number of government bilingual schools through face-to-face teaching in the target language to 100% of students for a minimum of 30% (7.5 hours) and up to 50% (12.5 hours) per week. The DBP is managed by the Languages Unit, Curriculum and Teaching Practice Division. Schools with the following school types are included in the DBP: The department may provide an opportunity for a new school to enter the Designated Bilingual Program, subject to Ministerial approval and the availability of resources. This involves a targeted Expression of Interest (EOI) process to a small number of schools who can demonstrate strong community support, existing strong language programs such as highly successful Content and Language Integrated Learning (CLIL) programs. Applicant schools must demonstrate an evidence-based pathway toward achieving 100% student participation in a bilingual program, with instruction time in the target language to be offered for a minimum of 30% (7.5 hours) per week and ranging through to an aspirational target of 50% (12.5 hours).
